metadata.name: Pilot subset of decoupleRpy Agent Evaluation Question Bank
metadata.scope: DecoupleRpy specialist only. Every question is expected to route through the coordinator to the 'decouplerpy' specialist agent. Routing correctness (reaching DecoupleRpy) is graded automatically; response quality is graded by an LLM judge against expected_behavior. Broader multi-specialist or direct-response routing tests are out of scope for this bank.

questions.question: Run ULM with CollecTRI on tcga_paad to estimate transcription-factor activities across all tumors and return the activity matrix.
Compute TF activities with mlm on paca_au_rnaseq and group results by Bailey subtype.
Run ulm with PROGENy on puleo_2018 and summarize pathway activity per the 5 subtypes.
On tcga_paad, run GSEA against Hallmark sets using a ranked logFC list I provide.
I want to compare TF activities between basal and classical PDAC subtypes. Which dataset, and run it.
questions.notes: Moffitt is the canonical classical/basal source.

questions.question: Estimate TF activities on the largest curated PDAC RNA-seq cohort.
questions.notes: TCGA-PAAD is the largest curated RNA-seq set.

questions.question: Which pathways look associated with worse survival in PDAC? Compute the activities I'd need.
questions.notes: Pick a survival cohort, infer activities; should note that the survival STATISTICS themselves are downstream of decoupleR.

questions.question: I have no dataset preference - just give robust TF activities on a big cohort.
questions.notes: Should pick a large cohort and explain.

questions.question: Use gse28735_pdac to compare the 4 Bailey molecular subtypes.
questions.notes: gse28735 is a 45-pair T/N design with no Bailey subtype labels; redirect to PACA-AU.

questions.question: Give me absolute TPM/counts from gse71729_moffitt before running ULM.
questions.notes: Microarray yields intensities, not counts/TPM; explain that decoupleR can still run on array intensities.

questions.question: Build a well-powered tumor-vs-normal contrast in tcga_paad.
questions.notes: TCGA-PAAD has very few normal samples; underpowered for T/N. Redirect to a matched-pair array set.

questions.question: Run CNV-aware TF-activity inference on tcga_paad using copy-number to weight regulons.
questions.notes: decoupleR uses the expression matrix only; no CNV layer is registered and CNV-weighting isn't a decoupleR feature.

questions.question: Run decoupleR on the Chan-Seng-Yue PDAC dataset.
questions.notes: TRAP: Chan-Seng-Yue was replaced by gse50827_nones and is NOT registered. Agent should say so and offer the replacement.

questions.question: Use a single-cell PDAC atlas to score cell-type-specific TF activity.
questions.notes: All 17 datasets are bulk; no single-cell.

questions.question: Run kinase-activity inference on the CPTAC phosphoproteomics layer.
questions.notes: Only the CPTAC RNA-seq TPM layer is registered; phosphoproteomics is not available.

questions.question: Fit a Kaplan-Meier curve and report the log-rank p-value for high vs low TF activity.
questions.notes: BOUNDARY: decoupleR produces the activity scores but does not run survival statistics. Best response: produce the activities and redirect KM/log-rank to a survival package (lifelines/survival). Grade redirect-with-explanation as a pass.

questions.question: Deconvolve bulk tumor samples into cell-type fractions.
questions.notes: BOUNDARY: deconvolution is not core decoupleR; redirect to CIBERSORTx/BisqueRNA. Note it can score marker-based gene sets per sample, which is related but not deconvolution.
